questions.question: On which date did the city of Paris enter a state of alarm, leading to the storming of the Bastille?
questions.explanation: The city of Paris was in a state of alarm on the morning of 14 July 1789, the day the Bastille was stormed. [cite: 36, 41]

questions.question: The Bastille was hated by the French people because it stood for:

options.C: The despotic power of the king

questions.explanation: The Bastille was hated because it was a symbol of the king's despotic power. [cite: 43]

questions.question: In 1774, who ascended the throne of France?
questions.explanation: Louis XVI of the Bourbon family became the King of France in 1774. [cite: 60]

questions.question: Which princess was Louis XVI married to?
questions.explanation: Louis XVI was married to the Austrian princess Marie Antoinette. [cite: 61]

questions.question: French society in the eighteenth century was divided into how many estates?
questions.explanation: The French society was organized into a system of three estates. [cite: 70]

questions.question: Which estate in French society was the only one that paid taxes?
questions.explanation: Only the members of the third estate were required to pay taxes. [cite: 70, 83]

questions.question: What was the 'tithe' in 18th-century France?

options.A: A tax paid to the King
options.B: A tax levied by the Church

questions.explanation: Tithe was a tax levied by the church, comprising one-tenth of agricultural produce. [cite: 81, 97]

questions.question: The direct tax to be paid to the state was called:
questions.explanation: Taille was the name for the direct tax paid to the state. [cite: 82, 98]

questions.question: The term 'Old Regime' is generally used to describe French society before:
questions.explanation: The term Old Regime describes the society and institutions of France before 1789. [cite: 72]

questions.question: What was the staple diet of the majority of people in France?
questions.explanation: Bread was the staple diet of the majority in France. [cite: 124]

questions.question: Which philosopher wrote the book 'The Social Contract'?
questions.explanation: Rousseau proposed a government based on a social contract in his book. [cite: 156, 201]

questions.question: Who proposed a division of power within the government between the legislative, executive, and judiciary?
questions.explanation: In 'The Spirit of the Laws', Montesquieu proposed the division of power into three branches. [cite: 157]

questions.question: On which day did Louis XVI call the meeting of the Estates General to pass new tax proposals?
questions.explanation: Louis XVI called together an assembly of the Estates General on 5 May 1789. [cite: 192]

questions.question: Where did the representatives of the Third Estate assemble on 20 June 1789 to take their famous oath?
questions.explanation: They assembled in the hall of an indoor tennis court in the grounds of Versailles. [cite: 204]

questions.question: Who wrote the influential pamphlet 'What is the Third Estate'?
questions.explanation: Abbé Sieyès, a priest, wrote the pamphlet 'What is the Third Estate?'. [cite: 231]

questions.question: In which year did the National Assembly complete the draft of the French Constitution?
questions.explanation: The National Assembly completed the draft of the constitution in 1791. [cite: 285]

questions.question: According to the Constitution of 1791, who were 'active citizens'?

options.A: All men and women in France
options.B: Men above 25 years who paid a certain amount of taxes
options.D: Only the members of the first two estates

questions.explanation: Active citizens were men above 25 who paid taxes equal to at least 3 days of a labourer's wage. [cite: 311]

questions.question: The 'Marseillaise' is the name of France's:
questions.explanation: The patriotic song Marseillaise is now the national anthem of France. [cite: 413]

questions.question: Which political club's name was derived from the former convent of St Jacob in Paris?
questions.explanation: The Jacobin club got its name from the former convent of St Jacob. [cite: 419]

questions.question: The 'sans-culottes' literally meant those:
questions.explanation: Sans-culottes literally means 'those without knee breeches'. [cite: 444]

questions.question: France was declared a Republic on 21 September of which year?
questions.explanation: On 21 September 1792, the assembly abolished monarchy and declared France a republic. [cite: 450]

questions.question: The period from 1793 to 1794 is known as the:
questions.explanation: The period from 1793 to 1794 is referred to as the Reign of Terror. [cite: 460]

questions.question: The guillotine was a device used for:
questions.explanation: The guillotine is a device with two poles and a blade used for beheading. [cite: 462]

questions.question: In which year did women in France finally win the right to vote?
questions.explanation: Women in France finally won the right to vote in 1946. [cite: 549]

questions.question: When was slavery finally abolished in the French colonies?
questions.explanation: Slavery was finally abolished in French colonies in 1848. [cite: 609]
